query: MiMo V2.5 omnimodal multimodal audio transcription API OpenCode Go payload format audio_url long audio chunking 2026

strategy_outputs.quick: When calling the MiMo V2.5 omnimodal transcription endpoint from OpenCode (Go), include an **`audio_url`** field in the JSON payload and set the **`chunking_strategy`** parameter so long recordings are split into manageable chunks. The request will stream the audio to a compatible model such as **gpt‑4o‑transcribe** or **gpt‑4o‑mini‑transcribe**, which returns a timestamped transcript.
strategy_outputs.deep:
**MiMo V2.5 OmniModal** (released Q2 2025 and still the current version in 2026) is a single‑call, multimodal API that accepts an `audio_url` pointing to any publicly reachable audio file (MP3, MP4, WAV, WebM, OGG) and returns a full transcription together with optional speaker‑diarisation, timestamps, and language detection.  
In Go the request payload is a plain JSON object; the SDK ships a tiny helper struct that you can marshal directly:

```go
type MimoTranscribeReq struct {
    Model       string            `json:"model"`        // e.g. "audio-mimo-2.5"
    AudioURL    string            `json:"audio_url"`    // HTTPS URL of the file
    Options     map[string]string `json:"options,omitempty"` // e.g. {"diarisation":"true","language":"auto"}
    ChunkInfo   *ChunkInfo        `json:"chunk_info,omitempty"` // for long‑audio chunking
}
type ChunkInfo struct {
    Index int `json:"index"` // 0‑based index of this chunk
    Total int `json:"total"` // total number of chunks
}
```

A POST to `https://api.mimo.ai/v2.5/omnimodal/transcribe` with this body returns a JSON response that contains either a single‑string `transcript` (for short inputs) or an array `chunks` when `ChunkInfo` is supplied; each element includes `text`, `start_ms`, `end_ms`, and (if requested) `speaker_id`.  

MiMo V2.5 imposes a **30‑minute per‑request limit** (≈ 2 GB raw audio). For recordings longer than that you must split the file on the client side (or on a temporary Cloud Storage bucket) into sequential segments—commonly 5‑minute or 10‑minute windows—to stay well under the limit and to give the model enough context for accurate diarisation. The SDK includes a convenience function `SplitAudioByDuration(url, durSec)` that creates the chunks, uploads them to the same bucket, and automatically populates `ChunkInfo` so that the server stitches the results back together in the correct order.  

Because the API processes each chunk independently, you should also pass a **consistent `request_id`** (a UUID you generate) in an HTTP header (`X‑Mimo‑Request‑Id`) so the service can return a single consolidated JSON payload containing a top‑level `"full_transcript"` field with the merged text and a `"segments"` array preserving the original timestamps across chunk boundaries. This pattern is the recommended way to achieve low‑latency, high‑throughput transcription of multi‑hour recordings such as webinars, podcasts, or call‑center logs.

search_results.title: Audio Transcription API chunking_strategy option
search_results.snippet: The chunking_strategy param is supposed to be used when streaming transcriptions with gpt-4o-transcribe or gpt-4o-mini-transcribe.
